text: The meaning of the Hebrew text is uncertain but the Latin version gives a clear sense. Ceremonial uncleanness could infect things as well as persons, and a body of soldiers must often have incurred the uncleanness which came from contact with a dead body (cf. Lev. 11.32). David assures the priest that his imaginary companions, although they have not set out to fulfil the conditions of a pious pilgrimage, are in fact free from defilement so far. Some think the word rendered ‘packs’ means ‘bodies’ here. Cf. Mt. 12.4.
